codeReview.summary: The core deletion work is thorough and correct. All 31 heatmap-related files were deleted (29 Swift/test files + 2 image assets), covering models, renderer, views, view models, services, tests, and assets across Core, iOS, and macOS targets. All 8 mixed files were properly cleaned: Enums.swift (wifiHeatmap case removed from enum and all computed properties), ServiceProtocols.swift (HeatmapDataPoint struct and WiFiHeatmapServiceProtocol removed), both ToolsView.swift files, ContentView.swift, AppSettings.swift, ToolsGoldenPathUITests.swift, and WiFiInfoService.swift (comment updated). RoomPlan.framework SDK dependency and NSCameraUsageDescription entries were removed from project.yml and both Info.plist files. EnumsTests.swift count updated from 19 to 18. Builds verified clean on both targets, 917 core tests pass, zero heatmap references remain in Swift files at commit time. The commit includes several out-of-scope additions (see issues) but none affect the correctness of the deletion work.

issues.description: Microsoft Word temp/lock file committed to repository. This file (162 bytes) is auto-generated by Word when a .docx is open and should never be version controlled. Should be removed and added to .gitignore (pattern: ~$*).

Brand new 148-line UI test file added in a commit scoped to 'delete all heatmap code'. This is completely out of scope for the clean-slate feature and should have been a separate commit. The test itself appears well-structured (captures 10 App Store screenshots) but doesn't belong in this change.

New WiFi Heatmap PRD documents (docx + pdf) added in a deletion commit. While useful for the mission, adding new heatmap-related documents in a 'clean slate deletion' commit is incongruent. Should have been a separate commit or added by the orchestrator.

Major rewrite of project AGENTS.md (201 lines changed) included in the deletion commit. The changes improve the file (better test commands, lint instructions, code style docs) but this is scope creep for a clean-slate feature. Should have been a separate commit.

26 lines of beads configuration appended to .beads/config.yaml. Includes a duplicate header comment '# .beads/config.yaml' and appears to be an entirely new config block appended after the existing placeholder content. Out of scope for heatmap deletion.

iOS target scheme configuration with testTargets added (NetMonitor-iOSTests, NetMonitor-iOSUITests). While useful, this was not requested in the feature description and is scope creep. The generated NetMonitor-iOS.xcscheme (117 lines) is a side effect of this change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker committed a Word temp file (docs/~$Fi-Heatmap-PRD.docx) to the repo. The project's .gitignore does not have a pattern for Word temp files (~$*). This is a gap in the gitignore configuration that should be documented or fixed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show bf8feaf --name-only --diff-filter=A shows docs/~$Fi-Heatmap-PRD.docx (162 bytes) as a new file. .gitignore has no ~$* pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker bundled unrelated changes (AGENTS.md rewrite, .beads/config.yaml, AppStoreScreenshotUITests.swift, WiFi-Heatmap-PRD docs, iOS scheme config) into a feature commit scoped to 'delete all heatmap code'. The mission AGENTS.md and skill don't explicitly instruct workers to keep commits atomic to their assigned feature scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: bf8feaf commit has 51 files changed: 31 deletions (in-scope) + 8 mixed-file edits (in-scope) + 5 new files and 2 config rewrites (out-of-scope). Commit message only describes the deletion work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that swift-core-worker skill's TDD step doesn't account for deletion-only features. The worker reasonably skipped TDD red/green phases since there's no new code to test. skillFeedback.suggestedChanges recommends updating the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Skipped TDD since this is a deletion-only feature with no new code to test'. suggestedChanges: 'The swift-core-worker skill's TDD step should mention that deletion-only features can skip the red/green phases'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered mac-mini git HTTPS authentication is broken (credential error -25308). Used local swift test as fallback. This was documented as a Known Pre-Existing Issue in the mission AGENTS.md, which is good. The services.yaml test commands reference SSH to mac-mini but no fallback commands are documented there.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0]: 'mac-mini git authentication is broken'. handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[1]: used local swift test. Mission AGENTS.md already documents this under 'Known Pre-Existing Issues'.

summary: PASS. The core deletion work is complete and correct — all 31 heatmap files deleted, all 8 mixed files properly cleaned, RoomPlan.framework and camera usage descriptions removed, both targets build cleanly, 917 tests pass, zero heatmap references remain in Swift files. Six non-blocking issues identified, all related to out-of-scope additions bundled into the commit (Word temp file, new UI tests, PRD docs, AGENTS.md rewrite, beads config, iOS scheme). None of these affect the correctness of the heatmap deletion or the build state.
